headline: Yishun is better framed as a value town than as the outright cheapest town in Singapore.
description: Condensed policy evidence translated into frontend-friendly cards and chart-ready data.

cards.label: Adjusted Yishun effect
cards.context: Estimated controlled price-per-sqm effect relative to the reference town.

cards.label: Adjusted town rank
cards.context: Rank after controlling for flat type, age, and year.

cards.label: Within-type floor-area slope
cards.context: Average within-flat-type completion-year slope from the policy analysis.

cards.label: Primary flat types
cards.context: Most important flat types highlighted in the current policy narrative.

insights: Yishun is better framed as a value town than as the outright cheapest town in Singapore.
Newer completions tend to be smaller in larger flat types, but the pattern is not universal across all flat categories.
The dashboard contract keeps explanatory text alongside quantitative metrics for static-site publishing.
